incidents.vector: AI-assisted reverse engineering of closed-source GitHub binaries (Wiz using IDA MCP) found CVE-2026-3854, an 8.7-CVSS push-options sanitization flaw allowing RCE for users with repository push access.
incidents.impact: GitHub patched github.com and confirmed no exploitation; on-prem Enterprise Server customers (88% still vulnerable at disclosure) must upgrade to fixed versions. Notable as one of the first critical bugs found in closed-source binaries via AI reverse engineering.

incidents.victim: Sergei K. (Kazakh national) — German defence industry / Ukraine aid
incidents.vector: Human intelligence operation: suspect allegedly fed Russia details on German military aid to Ukraine, drone/robotics defense firms, photos of public buildings and NATO military convoys, and offered to recruit further sources from Berlin since at least May 2025.
incidents.impact: Federal prosecutors arrested the suspect in Berlin; investigation continues. Incident underscores sustained Russian collection against Germany's Ukraine support and arms industry.

incidents.vector: Attackers compromised a school district employee's email account and impersonated the board of education, redirecting local school tax transfers to attacker-controlled accounts.
incidents.impact: $4.8 million in taxpayer funds stolen; the borough has been forced to issue short-term notes to cover the shortfall while attempting recovery.

incidents.victim: Vimeo and other Anodot customers (incl. McGraw Hill, ADT, Rockstar Games, Carnival)
incidents.vector: Voice and email phishing of an Anodot employee yielded authentication tokens for the analytics vendor, which the criminals used to pivot into the cloud environments of more than a dozen downstream customers without breaching them directly.
incidents.impact: Vimeo confirmed user/customer data including titles, metadata and some emails were exfiltrated. ShinyHunters added Vimeo to its leak site, threatened publication, and tied earlier theft from Rockstar to the same Anodot supply-chain compromise; 7.5M Carnival customer records were leaked from the same campaign.

incidents.vector: Infostealer (delivered as a Roblox cheat) infected an employee at AI vendor Context.ai, granting attackers access to Context.ai's Google Workspace and a pivot into Vercel's cloud infrastructure where credentials were stolen.
incidents.impact: Customer data and infrastructure secrets stolen and now being auctioned online; Vercel notified affected customers and recommended rotating keys and secrets, with fallout still expanding to additional customers and third-party systems.

incidents.vector: Unauthorized intrusion into the finance ministry's computer systems diverted an outbound foreign debt-repayment transfer originally destined for Australia.
incidents.impact: $2.5 million stolen in confirmed theft, with a further $600,000 payment to the US Postal Service unaccounted for. Sri Lanka has asked Australian law enforcement to assist; raises sharper questions about state financial controls amid post-default recovery.

incidents.attacker: China — Ministry of State Security contractor (Shanghai Powerock)
incidents.vector: Pandemic-era exploitation of zero-day vulnerabilities in Microsoft Exchange Server to implant webshells for persistent remote access; campaign targeted infectious-disease researchers, law firms, universities, defense contractors and policy think tanks.
incidents.impact: Xu Zewei was extradited from Italy to the US and arraigned in Houston on wire fraud, computer intrusion and aggravated identity theft charges (up to 62 years). DOJ describes him as one of many Chinese contractors used to obscure state cyber operations.

incidents.vector: Information-stealing malware distributed as fake gameplay-advantage software harvested player credentials, which a 19-year-old Ukrainian and accomplices then resold via Russian-domain marketplaces.
incidents.impact: Roughly $227,000 in proceeds from sales of high-value accounts containing rare in-game items and virtual currency. Ukrainian police detained the suspects, who face up to 15 years; investigation continues.

incidents.victim: Federal civilian agencies and Cisco ASA / Firepower / Secure Firewall users
incidents.vector: Advanced persistent threat actor exploited CVE-2025-20333 and CVE-2025-20362 in Cisco ASA firmware to deploy FIRESTARTER malware, which establishes post-patching persistence on Firepower and Secure Firewall devices, surviving firmware upgrades.
incidents.impact: CISA, NCSC-UK and ACSC issued joint advisories and an updated Emergency Directive 25-03 requiring forensic data collection and new vendor updates across federal agencies. Patching alone does not remediate; affected organizations must hunt for the implant.

incidents.victim: Critical infrastructure and government targets globally
incidents.vector: China-linked actors increasingly route the entire kill chain (reconnaissance, malware delivery, C2, exfiltration) through large covert botnets built on compromised SOHO routers and IoT devices, provisioned by Chinese information-security companies.
incidents.impact: NCSC and 15 international partners released joint guidance on defending against the technique, citing 'IOC extinction' that defeats static IP block lists and naming Chinese firms (e.g., Integrity Technology Group) as covert-network operators.

incidents.victim: Twilio, LastPass, DoorDash, Mailchimp and others (2022 attack spree)
incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — Scattered Spider / The Com
incidents.vector: SMS phishing campaigns and IT-help-desk social engineering harvested employee credentials at major tech firms; data was then used to run SIM-swap attacks against cryptocurrency investors.
incidents.impact: Tyler 'Tylerb' Buchanan pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft, admitting he stole at least $8M in crypto from individual victims; he faces up to 22 years at sentencing on Aug 21, 2026.

incidents.vector: AI hobbyists used a contractor's leaked credentials and guessed Mythos operating endpoints to gain access on the model's launch day.
incidents.impact: Anthropic says the intruders only 'played around' and did not run malicious prompts; serves as a warning about credential hygiene as agencies including Treasury request federal access to the model.

incidents.victim: Bundestag President Julia Klöckner / German political leadership
incidents.vector: Russian operators compromised Klöckner's Signal account, gaining access to a sensitive group chat that included Chancellor Friedrich Merz; consistent with a broader Russian campaign against Signal and WhatsApp accounts of European officials.
incidents.impact: German officials confirmed eavesdropping on senior-level conversations. Chancellor Merz's own account was not breached, but the incident exposed top-tier political comms and underscores Russia's interest in continental decision-making.

incidents.vector: Unidentified attackers breached the agency that manages French identity documents, driving licenses and vehicle registrations and put the data up for sale.
incidents.impact: France confirmed the breach; volume of compromised PII not yet disclosed but creates downstream identity-fraud risk for French citizens.

incidents.vector: Targeted OT malware ('ZionSiphon') geofenced to Israeli IPs and specific water-treatment software strings, designed to push pump pressure and chlorine dosing to dangerous levels.
incidents.impact: Darktrace found the malware fails to execute due to a XOR encoding error in its IP check, but the intent was to poison water in Tel Aviv and Haifa. No confirmed in-the-wild deployment; politically charged strings strongly suggest Iranian authorship.

incidents.vector: Second compromise in a month of the Checkmarx KICS vulnerability scanner: malicious versions of the KICS Docker image, GitHub Action and VS Code extensions were published to inject implants downstream.
incidents.impact: Bitwarden CLI confirmed as the first major downstream victim of this round; TeamPCP's announced partnership with Vect ransomware extends the threat from theft to encryption against thousands of downstream consumers.

incidents.vector: Unspecified intrusion exposed records the volunteers had donated for medical research; data subsequently listed for sale on Alibaba marketplace.
incidents.impact: Personal information of approximately 500,000 medical-research volunteers offered for sale, putting sensitive health data and identity at long-term risk.

incidents.vector: Unknown intrusion vector; the city took its IT network offline on a Friday and surrounding Leon County preemptively isolated its own systems.
incidents.impact: Multiple city websites and public services remain unavailable; defensive posture and severance language are consistent with a ransomware event, though officials had not formally confirmed at time of reporting.

incidents.vector: Attackers exploited a vulnerability in the Kelp DAO platform to withdraw funds as rsETH (restaked Ether) tokens.
incidents.impact: Approximately $292 million in crypto-assets stolen, the largest crypto-heist of 2026 so far. Recovery prospects unclear; incident extends a wave of DeFi exploits totaling more than $606M in 18 days.

incidents.victim: Ukrainian prosecutors and militaries of Greece, Romania and Serbia
incidents.vector: Targeted email compromise campaign against more than 170 Ukrainian prosecutors and partner militaries to gather investigative information; attributed by researchers to the GRU's APT28 unit.
incidents.impact: Sensitive prosecutorial and military email contents accessed; campaign first surfaced in March by Ctrl-Alt-Intel. Demonstrates continued Russian collection against Ukraine's legal and allied military targets.

incidents.victim: Iran-aligned cyber operations against US/Israeli OT/ICS
incidents.vector: Cluster CL-STA-1128 (publicly Cyber Av3ngers) shifted from Unitronics PLCs to Rockwell Automation / Allen-Bradley OT and SCADA gear, installing FactoryTalk software on attacker VPS infrastructure to enable exploitation against ~5,600 internet-exposed devices globally.
incidents.impact: CISA issued an aligned advisory on Apr 7. Iranian threat groups have resumed activity over Starlink VSAT after a 47-day domestic internet blackout, with Xpanse seeing Iranian-IP services jump from ~20K to ~300K daily since Apr 8.

incidents.vector: March cyberattack of unspecified vector disrupted shipping, invoicing and financial-reporting systems.
incidents.impact: Hasbro delayed its Q1 2026 financial results because impacted systems were still being restored; ongoing operational and reporting disruption at a major US toy maker.

incidents.vector: Internal corporate systems were accessed via unspecified means; details not disclosed.
incidents.impact: Itron disclosed and contained the incident on Apr 13; cyber insurance is expected to cover most costs. Notable because Itron technology underpins US grid and water-metering deployments.

incidents.vector: Pro-Russian hacktivist group (linked by Swedish officials to Russian security services) attempted to disrupt the plant's operations; intrusion was caught by the plant's built-in safeguards.
incidents.impact: No operational disruption to the plant; the incident, disclosed in mid-April, is one of several recent Russian probes of European energy infrastructure and reinforces the cyber-physical risk to the grid.
